question: I am a 26-year-old woman recently diagnosed with type 1 diabetes after sudden weight loss and fatigue. I have started insulin, but I am scared of long-term complications.
context_question: 26-year-old woman diagnosed with type 1 diabetes, experiencing sudden weight loss and fatigue, seeking advice on balancing insulin with meals and exercise.
answer: Type 1 diabetes management involves balancing insulin with meals and exercise. Learn carbohydrate counting to match insulin doses with carbohydrate intake. Consult your diabetes team for personalized guidance.

question: I am 16 weeks pregnant and hypothyroid. I am currently taking 75 mg per day of Eutirox (Synthroid) during weekdays and 100 mg per day on the weekends.
context_question: 16 weeks pregnant, hypothyroid, taking Eutirox (Synthroid) 75 mg on weekdays and 100 mg on weekends, inquiring about thyroid function test results.

Common reasons for the delay include hormonal imbalance, weight changes, stress, and thyroid dysfunction. Investigations like urine pregnancy test and ultrasound are suggested.

context_question: Initially believed to be eczema with severe itching. Affected areas include arms, hands, elbows, and face. Suspected jock itch after viewing images.

question: I have a condition involving a pimple-like bump or wart on my wrist, although I am not sure exactly what it is. It has been present for more than five years.
context_question: Pimple-like bump or wart on wrist for over five years. Painless, not growing. Used corn cap which removed surface but bump remains.

context_question: Unprotected sexual contact with commercial sex worker six months ago. Developed fever and body pain after 30 days. Widal test and urine test done, results showed typhoid.

question: I have pain and swelling in the corners of my big toe. It started one month ago.

answer: Symptoms suggest ingrown toenail with surrounding inflammation, acute paronychia. Granulation tissue formation at nail corner causing pain.

question: I have intense itching in my private part and redness.

question: Two months ago, my wife delivered a baby boy. 10 days before, we brought the baby home from my wife's place.

context_question: Blister on finger, nipples, and around eyes for about a week. Have eczema and taking Triamcinolone ointment.

question: I am 31 years old. A few weeks back, it started to itch under my foreskin.
context_question: 31 years old, itching under foreskin for a few weeks. Visited family doctor, blood sugar fine. Took L-Cin OZ, Zocon 150, avoided Candiderma.

sympt: small, round, red, and white painless spots

question: I noticed a red spot (possibly a rash) that has spread to a nearby mole in my groin area near the left thigh. It does not hurt, but it sometimes feels itchy.
context_question: Red spot possibly a rash in groin area near left thigh, sometimes itchy, slight burning sensation when washing with soap. Using over-the-counter Clotrimazole.

question: My mother had gallbladder surgery about seven months ago. She was also prescribed antibiotics such as Linezolid.
context_question: Mother had gallbladder surgery seven months ago, prescribed Linezolid. Developed dry mouth and oral candidiasis one month later. Symptoms resolved after antifungal treatment but returned.
answer: Symptoms are consistent with recurrent oral candidiasis due to persistent dry mouth, likely from the use of antibiotics like Linezolid. Antifungal treatment helps but symptoms may recur without addressing dry mouth.

question: I have a cluster of boils in the genital area, which has been there for over three months, and nothing seems to work to get them to disappear. I also have boils and rashes on my chest.
context_question: Cluster of boils in genital area for over three months, also boils and rashes on chest. Recently completed STI test, all negative.

question: I have been diagnosed with balanitis. Can you help with the irritation to go away?
context_question: Diagnosed with balanitis for five months, using steroid cream, antibiotics, and steroid creams like Hydrocortisone, Clotrimazole, and Betamethasone, but irritation persists.

question: I am a 30-year-old male. My height is 6 feet 2 inches, and my weight is 200 lbs.

question: I am on holiday. I wish to delay my period.

question: I am sexually active, and we do not use protection. I am currently 23 days late on my period, which has never happened before.

question: I missed multiple birth control pills for three days and started taking them again. I had unprotected sex, but I also missed a couple of pills a few days ago, which I took the following morning, and missed them again the next day. Is it fine? Kindly suggest.
context_question: Missed multiple birth control pills for three days, had unprotected sex, missed more pills. Seeking advice.
answer: Missing multiple pills means your protection was not reliable. There is a real possibility of pregnancy, especially since ovulation can happen when pills are missed frequently.

question: I am 23 and trying to find the right birth control, but everything seems to cause problems. I was on combination pills for two years, but developed high blood pressure that went up to 152/94 mm Hg.
context_question: 23 years old, trying to find suitable birth control. Developed high blood pressure (152/94 mm Hg) on combination pills.
answer: Avoid estrogen-containing contraception due to high blood pressure. Consider non-hormonal or very low hormone options.

question: My 29-year-old sister keeps getting these awful urticaria outbreaks that come and go without any warning. The hives cover her arms, legs, and sometimes her face, which is really embarrassing.
context_question: 29-year-old sister experiences urticaria outbreaks covering arms, legs, and face. Started about four months ago after switching birth control pills.
answer: Chronic urticaria can be distressing and often has no identifiable trigger. Hormonal changes may influence urticaria. Daily antihistamines like Cetirizine are the first-line treatment.

question: My migraine headaches have become absolutely debilitating since I started birth control pills four months ago, and I am wondering if there is a connection.
context_question: Migraine headaches debilitating since starting birth control pills four months ago. Never had migraines before.
answer: Combined oral contraceptives containing estrogen can trigger new-onset migraines or worsen existing ones. It is advised to stop estrogen-containing methods in this situation.

context_question: Taking Yasmin for six weeks, skipped placebo pills due to heavy bleeding and cysts. Experiencing breakthrough bleeding for seven days.
answer: Breakthrough bleeding can be normal when starting a new oral contraceptive, especially if placebo pills are skipped. This is common in the early phase of hormonal contraceptives.

question: I missed four doses of my birth-control pills and had unprotected sex about seven days ago. I have been having brown-colored blood or discharge since then.
context_question: Missed four doses of birth-control pills, had unprotected sex, experiencing brown-colored blood or discharge. Negative pregnancy test.

question: I am a 35-year-old woman diagnosed with chronic immune thrombocytopenia two years ago. My platelet count usually stays around 40,000 to 60,000, but recently I have had more bruises and heavy menstrual bleeding that lasts almost nine to 10 days.
context_question: 35-year-old woman with chronic immune thrombocytopenia, platelet count 40,000 to 60,000. Experiencing more bruises and heavy menstrual bleeding lasting nine to 10 days.

context_question: My 19-year-old daughter had her first seizure two months ago during finals. She fell out of her chair and started shaking.
answer: Epilepsy is diagnosed after one or more unprovoked seizures. With two events and epileptiform activity in sleep on EEG, the diagnosis is likely appropriate. Levetiracetam 500mg twice daily can cause depression and requires careful management.

question: I am 26 years old and have been on Yaz Plus for a few years. I started spotting after two days of my period's end date.

question: I am 35 years old and started using Nordette last month. However, I have been experiencing vaginal dryness and a lack of libido since then.
context_question: I am 35 years old and have been experiencing vaginal dryness and a lack of libido since starting Nordette.

context_question: My 29-year-old daughter started having seizures six months ago after starting birth control pills. She has had five grand mal seizures.

question: I am 24 years old and have been diagnosed with PCOS (polycystic ovarian syndrome). I have been taking Metformin (Dimet 500) for the past four months.

question: I took abortion pills under clinical guidance two months ago. However, it failed twice for a six-week pregnancy, and I had to undergo D&C (dilation and curettage) last month.

answer: After a D&C, it usually takes about four to six weeks for your next menstrual period to arrive. Count from the date of the D&C procedure.

question: I had brown spotting but no periods, and I do not know when I went to pee and wipe myself; the tissue paper has a dark brown color. I have symptoms like heavy breasts, and I feel very emoti.

answer: Brown spotting can occur when blood takes longer to exit the body. Symptoms like breast tenderness and fatigue may be related to hormonal changes.

question: I had unprotected sex two weeks ago, and I have not experienced withdrawal bleeding. I took I-pill within four hours after sex.

answer: I-pill is effective but not 100% guaranteed. If no periods occur within 14 days, a urine pregnancy test is recommended.

question: I have a question. A month back, my girlfriend started taking 21-day birth control pills (Margylen 21/7).
context_question: Girlfriend started 21-day birth control pills and has not had her period.
answer: The pills suppress ovulation and can cause changes in the menstrual cycle. It's normal for the withdrawal bleed to be delayed or missed in the first month.

question: My withdrawal bleed has not started, and instead, I only have brown discharge when I wipe. I am a bit concerned because I usually start bleeding by now.

answer: Brown discharge instead of a period can indicate a thin uterine lining due to the pill. Pregnancy or infection should be ruled out.

question: My period has been delayed for two days. I want to get my period in one day.

answer: Hormones can be used to delay periods, but starting them too late may not be effective. Primolute N (Norethisterone) can help if taken in advance.

question: I am a 32-year-old married woman. I was taking Femilon for the past two months, but stopped this month.
context_question: 32-year-old woman stopped taking Femilon after two months. Experienced period lasting 6 days, had protected sex on days 5 and 6, and took i-pill. Experienced another bleeding episode from the 14th to 20th.
answer: You were not in your fertile window during intercourse, so there's no risk of pregnancy. The I-pill (Levonorgestrel) is effective and can induce withdrawal bleeding, which is what you experienced after taking it.

question: My girlfriend had four Misoprostol tablets yesterday. She had cramps and many other symptoms for two hours.

question: I had my last period on the 30th of last month. I have a 25-day cycle with 5 days of bleeding.
context_question: Last period was on the 30th, has a 25-day cycle. Had sex on the 6th, took an i-pill within six hours, and experienced light bleeding from the 12th to the 20th.
answer: Since you took the emergency contraceptive pill within 72 hours, there's no chance of pregnancy. The delay may be due to hormonal changes from the pill. The spotting was likely withdrawal bleeding.

question: I need your advice regarding my pill use during this cycle. My period started, and I began taking Krimson-35 for two days.
context_question: Started taking Krimson-35 for two days after period began, missed pills for four days, then took it daily for 16 days and had unprotected intercourse.
answer: Missing Krimson-35 for four days reduced contraceptive protection. However, after taking it correctly for over 14 days, the chance of pregnancy is very low.

question: I had unprotected sex with my partner a few days ago, and she had her period date before intercourse, but after the sex, she took an i-pill within three hours.
context_question: Had unprotected sex a few days ago, partner took i-pill within three hours after sex, experienced bleeding the next day.
answer: You had intercourse on a safe day, so there was no need for the emergency contraceptive pill, which may disturb her menstrual cycle. The bleeding should be considered her menstrual bleeding.

question: I am 21 years old. I had my period, which lasted for four days.
context_question: 21-year-old had a period lasting four days, had intercourse after period, took emergency contraceptive pill the next day, and experienced bleeding a week later.
answer: The bleeding is likely withdrawal bleeding from the emergency contraceptive pill, not a sign of pregnancy. You took the pill within the recommended time frame, which is effective in preventing pregnancy.

question: I took an I-pill within four hours of unprotected sex. I had withdrawal bleeding six to seven days after that, which lasted for four days.
context_question: Took I-pill within four hours of unprotected sex, had withdrawal bleeding six to seven days later lasting four days, but no period since.
answer: Since you took the I-pill promptly and had negative pregnancy tests, pregnancy is ruled out. The delay in your period is a common effect of the I-pill, which can disrupt your natural cycle.

question: I took Levonorgestrel, then I had withdrawal bleeding after a week.
context_question: Took Levonorgestrel, had withdrawal bleeding after a week, had unprotected sex two days later, and took Levonorgestrel again.
answer: The withdrawal bleeding is a hormonal response to Levonorgestrel. Your cycle may become unpredictable, and ovulation timing can vary after taking it.

question: I am a 22-year-old female. I had my period on the second of this month.
context_question: 22-year-old female had period on the 2nd, had unprotected sex on the 10th, took Unwanted 72 within an hour, experienced spotting and light bleeding after five days.

question: I am having withdrawal bleeding that has been occurring for the last 10 to 11 days after taking an Unwanted 72-hour emergency contraceptive pill.

context_question: Being treated for PCOS, prescribed Irozorb syrup, ovacare tablets, and Sysron-N. Missed Sysron pill on the 22nd and 23rd, got period.

context_question: Will I lose weight before and after ovulation? I have maintained a constant weight for two weeks and am taking antipsychotic medications.
answer: Weight changes around ovulation are usually not dramatic. Antipsychotic medications can slow metabolism and make weight loss harder. Two weeks without movement on the scale is not unusual; it could be fluid balance. Recommended tests: Thyroid function test, HbA1c.

context_question: I have been trying for a baby for two years with irregular periods and unpredictable ovulation.

question: I have frequent urination problem and have been trying to conceive. I also have swollen breasts and a dark line from my belly button to my vagina.
context_question: I have frequent urination, swollen breasts, and a dark line from my belly button to my vagina while trying to conceive.

answer: Your symptoms suggest hormonal imbalances from an anovulatory cycle. Ovulation may occur around the 18th day of your 42-day cycle, but it seems there are no chances of ovulation this cycle.

WBC - 13700, neutrophils - 76%, lymphocytes - 19%, eosinophils - 02%, monocytes - 03%, basophils - 0%, RBC- 4.5, HCT - 39.8, MCV - 84.5, MCH - 27, MCHC - 32, Hb - 12.5, platelets - 410000, and vitamin B12 - 184. WBC is above the maximum for over a period of one year now. Last year, when I was ill, I got some rashes on my hands and legs.

answer: A sugar level of 210 after eating is not normal and suggests diabetes mellitus. Repeat serum fasting blood sugar, post-prandial blood sugar, and HbA1c tests to confirm diabetes. Control can be achieved with weight reduction and exercise.

question: I am on Depot injection, and I have missed the shot for over ten days now. I have not started bleeding yet.

answer: Missing the shot means the effectiveness is lost. Get a serum beta hCG test done to check for pregnancy. Duphaston withdrawal may induce your periods.

question: I am a 17-year-old girl. I am taking a Depo-Provera injection.
context_question: I am a 17-year-old girl taking a Depo-Provera injection. Is it good for me?
answer: The Depo-Provera injection is effective for birth control and should be repeated every 12 weeks. Regular checkups are advised to monitor side effects.

question: I am normally around 33 days on average in my menstrual cycle, but today is day 35, and my period has not started yet.
context_question: I am on day 35 of my menstrual cycle, normally 33 days, and my period has not started. What could be causing the delay?
answer: A delay after stopping Depo is common. Rule out pregnancy with a home test. If negative, wait a few days; it may be due to hormone recovery.

question: My period started 12th of last month. I reviewed the Depo shot two days later.
context_question: Patient had a period starting on the 12th of last month, reviewed Depo shot two days later, had unprotected sex nine days later, and is experiencing ovulation symptoms and spotting.
answer: Depot injection does not offer immediate protection. Intermenstrual bleeding is a common side effect. Ovulation bleeding is possible this cycle. Barrier contraception is advised for 2 to 3 weeks post-injection. Pregnancy may be possible.
article_title: Spotting vs Period Explained: Causes, Symptoms And Medical Advice

question: I had unprotected intercourse with my partner while I was in my unsafe period. Although I took an ECP within 6 to 8 hours of time, I am a bit curious as if it will work or not.
context_question: Patient had unprotected intercourse during unsafe period and took an emergency contraceptive pill (ECP) within 6 to 8 hours.
answer: Taking the emergency contraceptive pill within 72 hours of intercourse significantly reduces the chance of pregnancy. If periods are delayed, a urine pregnancy test is advised.

question: I was on the Depo injection for four years. I have been off the Depo injection for one year and three months.
context_question: Patient was on Depo injection for four years, off for one year and three months, and has not taken other contraception. Hormone tests showed low levels.
answer: After stopping Depo, periods usually start within a year. If hormone levels are normal, periods should resume. Hormonal contraceptive tablets can be taken to induce periods.

question: I started Depo three months ago because the pill was leading to yeast infections . But every day since taking the shot birth control, I have had a brown discharge that feels like is causing another yeast infection. Will the discharge ever go away? What should I do?
context_question: Patient started Depo three months ago due to yeast infections and has had a brown discharge daily since then, suspecting another yeast infection.
answer: Depo may cause yeast infections. Use Candid-B V gel (Clotrimazole vaginal gel) and Fluconazole 150 mg once a week for five weeks. Consult a specialist for further advice.

question: I am a 22 year old female. On the 24th, I got an implant (rod) in my arm to prevent unwanted pregnancy.
context_question: 22-year-old female received an implant for pregnancy prevention and experienced non-stop bleeding for three months after.
answer: Implanon did not suit you. After removal, switching to Depo injection was immediate. Irregular periods are expected. Antifibrinolytics like Tranexamic acid 500 mg thrice daily can help stop bleeding.

question: I came off the Depo birth control injection about seven months ago. I have been trying to conceive , but nothing yet.
context_question: Patient stopped Depo injection seven months ago, trying to conceive but has prolonged periods and negative ovulation tests.
answer: Depo-Provera may take longer than four months to clear. Hormonal balance is not achieved yet. Combined oral birth control pills can help regulate cycles.

question: My cycle is about 25 days. Me and my husband got intimated on day 18 using a condom.
context_question: Patient had intercourse on day 18 of a 25-day cycle using a condom and took a morning-after pill within an hour.
answer: The morning-after pill is effective if taken within an hour. If the urine pregnancy test is positive, consult a doctor for medical termination of pregnancy. Depo-Provera is also suggested for contraception.

question: I am 25 years old, and I took the contraceptive injection this year. However, I have been experiencing bleeding for the past four months.
context_question: 25-year-old patient took a contraceptive injection this year and has had bleeding for four months.
answer: Bleeding may be due to a urinary tract infection or pelvic inflammatory disease. Coagulation agents and tests are recommended to identify the cause.

question: I had an abortion four months ago, I never went back to get checked out and it was a D and E. I am also on Depo-Provera, second dose.
context_question: Patient had a D and E abortion four months ago, is on second dose of Depo-Provera, and is experiencing dark brown discharge with clots.
answer: This is breakthrough bleeding, a common side effect of Depo-Provera. If bleeding is heavy, antifibrinolytics like Tranexamic acid can be used.

question: I had my first Depo injection shot four months back; before that, I had never used any birth control. I did not have my period for a while.
context_question: Patient had first Depo injection four months ago, did not have periods, and has been bleeding since the second shot.
answer: Heavy, irregular bleeding is a side effect of Depo-Provera. Continuous bleeding may lead to anemia. Monitoring is advised.
article_title: Pregnancy and Birth

question: My mother is 43 years old and received 250 mg Methylprednisolone daily for five days, seven months back, because of COVID. What are the long-term severe side effects that I should watch?
context_question: Patient's mother is 43 years old and received 250 mg Methylprednisolone daily for five days due to COVID.
answer: Steroids taken for longer durations can cause chronic side effects like diabetes, hypertension, and osteoporosis. Since it was only for five days, long-term effects are unlikely.

question: I have been suffering from health problems for the last five months. I had a lot of discomfort in my stomach and around my teeth.
context_question: Patient has been suffering from stomach discomfort and dental issues for five months, with a gynecologist suspecting endometriosis.
answer: Symptoms may indicate postural hypotension. Testing for cortisol levels and iron deficiency anemia is recommended. Stay hydrated and avoid herbal supplements.

question: My wife is a neuro patient and a week ago she was given five days treatment of Methylprednisolone intravenous. After her discharge, she has been vomiting every now and then.
context_question: Patient's wife is a neuro patient who received five days of intravenous Methylprednisolone and is now experiencing vomiting.
answer: Monitor for gastritis and gastric ulcers. Advise small frequent meals, avoid spicy foods, and consider Pantocid D (Domperidone and Pantoprazole) for nausea.

question: Since childhood, I have experienced joint pains. I had food aversion and was taken to the ER once for fainting with a high fever.
context_question: Patient has experienced joint pains since childhood, food aversion, and fainting with high fever.
answer: You may have polymyalgia rheumatica. A single dose of Depo-Medrol (Methylprednisolone Acetate) 40 mg is suggested for diagnosis confirmation and management.

question: I am a 28-year-old male. I have been diagnosed with RA.
context_question: 28-year-old male diagnosed with rheumatoid arthritis (RA) and using Methylprednisolone 4 mg daily.
answer: Long-term steroid use can mask symptoms and cause side effects like cataracts and osteoporosis. Blood tests and joint ultrasounds are recommended for further evaluation.
article_title: Osteoporosis: an Important Public Health Problem

question: I have developed red, inflamed, slightly raised lesions on the tip of the penis. The area feels slightly moist and irritated.
context_question: Patient has red, inflamed lesions on the penis after taking Methylprednisolone tablets for 20 days.
answer: This condition is balanitis. Wash the area with a cleanser and provide history of exposure, diabetes, or irritants for a complete treatment plan.
article_title: Balanitis - Types, Causes, Symptoms, Diagnosis, Treatment, and Complications

question: I got married before seven years and till I do not have a baby. My periods are regular in an interval of 28 days.
context_question: Patient has been married for seven years without conceiving, has regular 28-day cycles, and has not gotten periods this month despite taking Sysron NCR and Predmet 16 mg.
answer: The delay may be due to Predmet (Methylprednisolone), which is known to delay cycles. Wait for two weeks for drug clearance before further evaluation.

question: I am suffering from inflammatory myopathy confirmed by EMG, NCS and biopsy with CPK 2300 range and SGOT, SGPT also were little higher. I have already taken five days 1 g Prednisone stero
context_question: Suffering from inflammatory myopathy confirmed by EMG, NCS, and biopsy. CPK 2300, SGOT, SGPT slightly elevated. Took 1 g Prednisone for five days, then Omnicortil for 1-1.5 months.
answer: Inflammatory myopathy requires lifetime treatment. Steroids and immunosuppressive therapy control progression. Prednisolone is effective but has side effects; taper to the minimum. Discuss Methylprednisolone dosage based on symptoms and CPK levels.
article_title: GNE Myopathy - Causes, Diagnosis,Treatment, and Prognosis.

question: I have been suffering from a skin problem for a long time four years. They are white patches.
context_question: Suffering from a skin problem for four years with white patches. Treated by doctors with lotion and blue light therapy, but developed blisters.
answer: You can reach out for further assistance. Please consult a dermatologist for a proper evaluation and treatment plan for your skin condition.

question: I am 52 years old. I have had an issue with topical dermatitis or eczema for about a year and a half.
context_question: 52 years old with topical dermatitis or eczema for a year and a half. Using steroid and anti-itch creams, now experiencing hives covering most of the body.
answer: You have hives and dermatitis. Consider uploading allergy test reports. Gluten testing is recommended. Monitor the effects of Paroxetine and Bupropion on your symptoms.
article_title: Bupropion - Mechanism of Action, Indications, Contraindications, and Side Effects

question: I am on rebound congestion because of misuse of the nasal spray. I have used it for seven days, not knowing that it would cause me a rebound effect.
context_question: Experiencing rebound congestion from nasal spray misuse for seven days. ENT doctor advised to continue spray due to dry nasal congestion.
answer: Rebound congestion is temporary. Turbinates can swell and shrink. Continue with steroid sprays; monitor symptoms. Discuss with your doctor if congestion persists.
article_title: Turbinate Reduction Surgery - Risks, Indications, and Precautions

question: I conceived with the help of frozen embryo transfer , which was done the 26th of last month. My last menstrual period was on the seventh of last month.
context_question: Conceived via frozen embryo transfer on the 26th of last month. Last menstrual period was on the seventh. Beta hCG levels are increasing.
answer: Your beta hCG levels are normal. Continue prescribed medications. If taking blood thinners, consult your doctor about stopping them.

question: I am a patient of PCOD. I had my last periods before nine days.
context_question: Patient with PCOD, last period nine days ago. Took Letrozole 2.5 mg for five days. USG shows right ovary follicle 23.4 mm.
answer: Follicle size indicates high chances of ovulation in 24 hours. hCG dose is recommended today. Intercourse should follow the hCG injection for conception.

question: How to confirm pregnancy?
context_question: Asking how to confirm pregnancy.
answer: Get a urine pregnancy test with an early morning sample. For certainty, consider a blood test for pregnancy.

question: I am 3 months pregnant. My blood group is O negative.
context_question: 3 months pregnant, blood group O negative. Concerned about complications due to negative blood group. Taking Fertigyn 5000 injection weekly.
answer: Negative blood group may cause complications if partner is positive. Discuss anti-D injections with your obstetrician. Fertigyn is safe for maintaining pregnancy.
article_title: Anemia - Symptoms, Causes, Prevention, and Treatment

question: I have doubt regarding positive pregnancy after embryo transfer. My wife had an embryo transfer before 15 days.
context_question: Concern about positive pregnancy after embryo transfer 15 days ago. Wife took Ovidrel before transfer and Triptorelin injections after.
answer: Triptorelin can cause false positive hCG tests. Consult for further clarification.

question: I had PCOS. We have been trying to conceive for the past two years.
context_question: Had PCOS and trying to conceive for two years. Experienced one biochemical pregnancy and one missed miscarriage.
answer: Women with PCOS have a higher risk of abortion. Healthy lifestyle and weight management are crucial. Current medications are sufficient for pregnancy.

question: I have been trying to conceive for some time now. This month I was put on hCG injections and egg formation.
context_question: Trying to conceive, on hCG injections. Periods started three days early after a faint line on a pregnancy test.
answer: The faint line may be due to hCG injections. Consult if you have further doubts.

question: I am 34 years old and have been trying to conceive for second pregnancy for eight months. My first pregnancy was a natural conception.
context_question: 34 years old, trying to conceive second pregnancy for eight months. Undergoing ovulation induction with Progynova and hCG injections.
answer: Chances of fertilization are around 60%. Pregnancy test can be done 14-16 days after hCG injection.
article_title: In-Vitro Fertilization (IVF): An Overview

question: I completed my MDR tuberculosis treatment three months ago. Last month, I became pregnant and visited a gynecologist.
context_question: Completed MDR tuberculosis treatment three months ago. Pregnant last month, started HCG injections after TB treatment.
answer: HCG injections are generally safe after completing tuberculosis treatment, provided recovery is complete.
article_title: Management of MDR and XDR Tuberculosis: An Overview

question: I have a few questions. My gynecologist prescribed Nifedipine as folic acid.
context_question: Gynecologist prescribed Nifedipine as folic acid. Questions about its purpose and recent ultrasound findings.
answer: Nifedipine is not folic acid. Insufficient follicles may have affected implantation. Consider taking Letrozole and consult your doctor.

question: Me and my partner are trying to conceive. I was done with one cycle of Letrozole, my eggs were small so OB gave me shots of FSH.
context_question: Trying to conceive, completed Letrozole cycle. Received FSH shots and hCG shot for ovulation.
answer: Continue having sex regularly to increase chances of pregnancy. Hormonal balance is important; consider adding Bromocriptine and Manix.

question: I am 7 weeks pregnant. I am passing clots and spotting is there for the last two weeks.
context_question: 7 weeks pregnant, passing clots and spotting for two weeks. USG shows subchorionic hemorrhage.
answer: You are on progesterone. If bleeding decreases, continue current treatment. If not, consult your obstetrician.

question: I have been trying to conceive for many months. I was prescribed FSH 75 IU injection on the seventh, eighth and ninth day of my monthly cycle.
context_question: Trying to conceive, prescribed FSH 75 IU injections on specific days. Forgot first injection, asking if it can be taken later.
answer: You can take the FSH injection on the tenth day. Follow up with transvaginal sonography for egg maturation.

question: My LMP was five months ago. I was spotting for three days during my LMP and got a positive strip test after seven days.
context_question: LMP was five months ago, had spotting for three days, positive strip test after seven days, beta hCG level was 126, then 125. Advised to continue Folic acid and multivitamins, ultrasound in three weeks if no periods.
answer: Spotting in early pregnancy is normal, but a fall or no rise in beta hCG is a poor sign. It indicates fetal tissue may not be growing. Follow doctor's advice for a scan to confirm. Check for any problems if worried about miscarriages.

question: I am a woman aged 31 years, and I am planning to conceive for 10 months. My doctor gave me an HCG injection of 1000 IU, then Clomid, then Duphaston for 15 days.
context_question: 31-year-old woman planning to conceive for 10 months, received HCG injection of 1000 IU, Clomid, and Duphaston for 15 days. Experienced brown discharge after last dose.
answer: The brown sticky discharge is likely withdrawal bleeding after Duphaston. It may not be a full period. Do a pregnancy test to check if conceived. If bleeding changes or pain occurs, consult a doctor.
article_title: Withdrawal Bleeding

question: I am a 25-year-old woman trying to conceive for five months. I have taken treatment for the last two months after the follicular study and took hCG this month, but I did not get pregnant.
context_question: 25-year-old woman trying to conceive for five months, took treatment for two months after follicular study, received hCG this month but did not get pregnant.
answer: You are young and have only tried for five months. hCG is helpful for ovulation and early pregnancy support. Investigate further after a year of trying. Check semen analysis for husband and blood tests for both partners.

question: I am a 30-year-old female. I am six weeks pregnant and have an ovarian cyst.
context_question: 30-year-old female, six weeks pregnant, has an ovarian cyst, experienced light spotting, advised Isolan and alternate hCG injections, has UTI and constant uneasiness in the uterus.
answer: Corpus luteal cysts can support pregnancy and dissolve over time. Follow doctor's instructions and take rest. Treat UTI to avoid complications. Get a urine culture and take antibiotics as needed.
article_title: Pregnancy and Birth

question: I got my periods on the second before two months and then fourth of last month; then ninth of this month. So I assume that I have a 30 to 32 days cycle generally.
context_question: Assuming a 30 to 32 days cycle, had unprotected sex last week of the month, inquiring about chances of pregnancy and when to start contraceptive pills.
answer: You have a 10% - 20% chance of pregnancy after sex on the 18th day of a 32 to 34 day cycle. If you miss your period, do a urine or blood test. Start contraception on the second day of your period.

question: I am 41 years old. Last year I had erratic periods with prolonged heavy bleeding in some cycles.
context_question: 41-year-old woman, had erratic periods with prolonged heavy bleeding, prescribed Yasmin pills for three months, took for two months and felt good.
answer: Your periods have normalized, indicating Yasmin was effective. If periods remain unpredictable, you can continue Yasmin for three months. It provides additional contraception if not breastfeeding.

question: I have been taking Yasmin for a long time. I usually never miss any pills.
context_question: Long-term Yasmin user, got period on the fourth day of last pill, started new pack without waiting, experienced spotting for ten days and dizziness.
answer: Chances of pregnancy after sex on the seventh day of the last period are negligible due to Yasmin use. Yasmin prevents ovulation, making conception unlikely. Increasing progesterone with Crina NCR is not justified.
article_title: Norethisterone - Mechanism of Action, Uses, Contraindications, and Side Effects

question: I am 42 years old. I am on Yasmin for many years and was quite comfortable with it.
context_question: 42-year-old woman, on Yasmin for many years, got period on time, started new pack on second day of pill, still spotting on the ninth day.
answer: If spotting continues, do a urine pregnancy test. If negative, consult a gynecologist for examination and possible ultrasound. Provide details to assist further.

question: I am 40 years old. My last month period was on time but it lasted only one day.
context_question: 40-year-old woman, last period was on time but lasted one day, current bleeding for 12 days, advised to take OCP Yasmin.
answer: For 12 days of bleeding, start Trapic MF (500 mg three times a day for three days). If pregnancy test is negative, start Yasmin as prescribed. If positive, consult a gynecologist.
article_title: Role of Tranexamic Acid in Patients Undergoing Non-cardiac Surgery

question: I always feel tired and lethargic despite sleeping sufficiently, and I feel nauseous after eating.
context_question: Feeling tired and lethargic for six months, experiencing nausea after eating, previously low iron levels now normal, currently taking Yasmin.
answer: See a doctor for accurate diagnosis regarding tiredness and nausea. Consider quality of sleep and potential sleep disorders affecting your symptoms.
article_title: Psychiatric Manifestations of Vitamin B12 Deficiency

question: We had unprotected intercourse a few days back, and my wife took ipill after 24 hours.
context_question: Unprotected intercourse followed by ipill taken after 24 hours, inquiring if another ipill is needed after subsequent intercourse.
answer: Your wife need not take another ipill. It is not safe to use ipill repeatedly. Regular contraception is recommended, such as Yasmin or Yaz, which have fewer side effects.

question: I am a 23-year-old female; my weight is 51 kg, and my height is four feet and nine centimeters. I am not under medication.
context_question: 23-year-old female, weight 51 kg, height four feet nine inches, had unprotected intercourse, took three Yasmin tablets as emergency contraception.
answer: The bleeding after taking Yasmin is likely breakthrough bleeding. Emergency contraceptive pills are not as effective as regular contraceptive pills. Monitor for any unusual symptoms.
article_title: Drospirenone and Ethinyl estradiol - Uses, Dosage, and Side Effects

question: I am a 33-year-old woman. I have been suffering from PCOS for a long time.
context_question: 33-year-old woman, unmarried, suffering from PCOS, found an ovarian cyst during a doctor's visit, currently taking Gynera.
answer: The ovarian cyst is likely simple and will resolve in two to three menstrual cycles. Gynera can help with cyst regression. Watch for severe abdominal pain or heaviness.

question: I have PCOS. I want to know what happens to all these unreleased follicle eggs on ovaries?
context_question: Inquiring about unreleased follicle eggs in PCOS, experiencing spotting after periods, and how Yasmin helps.
answer: Unreleased follicles undergo apoptosis. Spotting may be due to hormonal imbalances. Yasmin regularizes cycles and is effective for PCOS patients.

question: I am taking the Yasmin contraceptive pill and get spotting after ten to fifteen days on the active pills.
context_question: Experiencing spotting after ten to fifteen days on Yasmin, inquiring about stronger pills to prevent bleeding.
answer: Yasmin is effective but bleeding after ten to fifteen days is abnormal. Get a sonography and pap smear to check for irregular bleeding causes. Consider switching to a higher pill if tests are normal.

question: My fiancee took four Yasmin pills as emergency contraception.
context_question: Fiancee took four Yasmin pills as emergency contraception, feeling weak and having heavy breathing, inquiring about effectiveness and side effects.
answer: Yasmin can be used for emergency contraception within 72 hours. Side effects may include nausea and headache. Consider anti-emetic before taking the regimen.

question: My fiancÃ©e and I had unprotected sex a few days back, but I did not ejaculate inside her. Just to be on the safer side, we wanted to use the emergency contraceptive pill.
context_question: Patient had unprotected sex a few days ago and wants to use emergency contraceptive pills, but they are not available.
answer: Yasmin can be used as an emergency pill. Take four tablets of Yasmin (Ethinyl Estradiol and Drospirenone) at once, then repeat four more tablets after 12 hours. This high dose will increase the inner lining of the uterus, making it unfavorable for a possible embryo.

question: I have read that the Yasmin pill can be used as an emergency morning-after pill. How many doses are required and when can we stop taking them?
context_question: Patient is asking about the dosage of Yasmin as an emergency pill after a condom slip.
answer: It is not essential to take pills for a condom slip unless sperm was released inside. Please provide details about the incident and your wife's menstrual cycle to assess the need for emergency contraception.

question: Myself and my wife had unprotected sex before two weeks. She told me that it was her sixth day after having her last period.
context_question: Patient's wife has soreness in her breast for three days after unprotected sex and is worried about pregnancy.
answer: Since it was the sixth day after her last period, she is nearing ovulation. Birth control pills taken now won't help. Emergency pills should be taken within 72 hours. If she misses her period, a urine pregnancy test is recommended.

question: I have been taking the contraceptive pill Yasmin for years. Recently, my pharmacy ran out of Yasmin and gave me Yasminelle.
context_question: Patient is experiencing bad bowel movements after switching from Yasmin to Yasminelle.
answer: Both pills have the same composition, but Yasminelle might cause symptoms. You can switch back to Yasmin. If symptoms persist, there may be another cause that needs to be treated.

question: My wife was diagnosed with SIBO a few months back. We changed our diet to compensate, and now she is on antibiotics.
context_question: Patient's wife has persistent gut pain and it affects her sex life.
answer: We need to consider other causes of abdominal pain. It could be gastritis or pelvic inflammatory infection. Antacids like Esomeprazole and Domperidone may help if gastritis is confirmed.

question: I am becoming very frustrated with my sex life. My husband and I have been married for three years.
context_question: Patient is frustrated with her sex life due to her husband's anxiety about sex.
answer: Differences in sexual desires can be addressed through counseling. Understanding the reasons behind these differences is essential for improvement.

question: My periods have been regular until this month. But now my period is late by six days.
context_question: Patient's period is late by six days and she has not been sexually active for five months.
answer: A delay in periods can be normal. It may be due to weight changes or stress. Check thyroid profile and serum prolactin. Hormonal imbalance can also cause missed periods.
article_title: Combined Oral Contraceptive Pills - Indications and Side Effects

question: I recently had a vacuum abortion in the first trimester three days ago and just had sexual intercourse two days later.
context_question: Patient is on antibiotics after a recent abortion and is worried about getting pregnant again.
answer: There is no chance of pregnancy immediately after an abortion due to lack of ovulation. No medication is needed, but be careful next time.

question: I am in my 9th week of pregnancy. My husband was out for a month and has returned now.
context_question: Patient is concerned about the safety of intercourse during her first pregnancy.
answer: Intercourse should be avoided until the 16th week of gestation to prevent miscarriage. Other ways to express emotions are recommended during this time.

question: I am a 22-year-old female, married a few days back. I do not have an interest in sex.
context_question: Patient lacks interest in sex and is experiencing misunderstandings with her husband.
answer: Understanding the reasons for lack of interest is crucial for suggesting a treatment plan.

question: Does Gelusil affect the efficacy of HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus) post-exposure prophylaxis?
context_question: Patient is asking if Gelusil affects HIV post-exposure prophylaxis.
answer: Syrup Gelusil does not affect the efficacy of HIV prophylaxis.

question: Five weeks ago, I had a hysterectomy along with uterus, cervix and fallopian tubes. I am experiencing light pink to red vaginal bleeding after dealing with high stress at work.
context_question: Patient is experiencing light vaginal bleeding after a hysterectomy and high stress.
answer: Post-hysterectomy bleeding can result from infection or stress. Reduce stress to allow healing and avoid rough vaginal sex.

sympt: light pink to red vaginal bleeding

question: I had urinal infection two weeks back. At that time the pus cells were 55.
context_question: Patient had a urinary infection and is currently unable to have sex due to burning.
answer: Burning after a urinary infection is common. Abstain from sex while on treatment. Your partner should complete the antibiotic course, and lubricants can help reduce pain during sex afterward.
article_title: How to Prevent Urinary Infection in Women?

question: How common is HPV in our country?
context_question: Patient is inquiring about the prevalence of HPV.
answer: HPV is highly prevalent and is the leading cause of cervical cancer in women. Over 75,000 women die from cervical cancer annually in our country.
article_title: Cervical Cancer - Causes and Prevention

question: After intercourse, I had to deal with burning pee. And although it has reduced stinging now.
context_question: Patient is experiencing burning after intercourse and is unsure about taking the pill after unprotected sex.
answer: During the safe period, there is no chance of pregnancy. These symptoms may indicate a urinary infection. Get checked and maintain hygiene.

question: I had sexual intercourse last night with protection. Immediately after, I went to take a shower and I see that my left labia majora is swollen but painless.
context_question: Patient has painless swelling of the left labia majora after intercourse.
answer: The swelling may be due to an allergic reaction to the condom or minor trauma. No medication is needed; local fomentation and sitz baths are recommended.

question: I had hiatal hernia surgery and Nissen fundoplication 13 days ago. I did not have sex with my girlfriend for the past 13 days.
context_question: Patient is asking if it's safe to have sex after surgery.
answer: Avoid sex for at least four to six weeks post-surgery. Foreplay without vigorous activities is allowed.

question: How to properly clean the vagina after period sex, to avoid any discomfort? Is it necessary to clean the vagina after engaging in period sex, if so, why?
context_question: Patient is asking about hygiene after period sex.
answer: After intercourse, wipe with tissue or wash with water if sticky. Avoid frequent washing and soap to maintain natural bacteria balance.

question: Hi, I was fingering my pussy, now it is burning a lot and I feel like scratching it.
context_question: Patient is experiencing burning and itching after fingering.
answer: Do not scratch. Use Ointment Vagisil locally and V-wash for daily hygiene. Scratching may worsen the discomfort.

question: I observed the presence of vaginal discharge almost a month ago and I went for a test. It showed the presence of WBCs, but no bacteria or yeast or parasite.
context_question: Patient has vaginal discharge for almost a month, completed antibiotics, still feels irritation and foul-smelling urine.
answer: The cause of excess white discharge and foul smell could be a urine infection. Get a urine test done. Use Doxy (Doxycycline) 100 mg twice a day for five days and Candid B cream for local application. Maintain personal hygiene and drink plenty of water.

question: I had a light green discharge yesterday, and I noticed it was not normal. When I searched online, I found that it could be an infection.
context_question: Patient has light green discharge, suspects infection, has not had intercourse recently.
answer: You likely have bacterial vaginosis. Please do a high vaginal and endo cervical swab test. Take Co-Amoxiclav 625 mg twice daily for two weeks and Metronidazole 400 mg thrice daily for two weeks.

Test: high vaginal and endo cervical swab test

question: I am on the last day of my first trimester and I was experiencing water-like discharge yesterday as if I am about to start periods. I had intercourse five to six days before this.
context_question: Patient is in the last day of first trimester, experiencing water-like discharge and itching on outer labia.
answer: This may indicate a vaginal infection. Avoid penetrative sex until the end of pregnancy. Take Azilide (Azithromycin) 500 mg once a day for three days and Taxim-O (Cefixime) 200 mg twice a day for five days.

question: I have a strong pain in my vagina. It is not a deep pain but on the inside of the opening.
context_question: Patient has strong pain in vagina, unbearable during intercourse, with increased discharge but no unusual odor.
answer: The issue seems to be friction-related injuries during intercourse, followed by infection. Avoid sexual activities for a few weeks. Use Clingen vaginal tablet once daily for 3 days and take Fluconazole 150 mg once per day for three days.

question: I have pain in the area between my vaginal lips and thigh, especially when I use soap to clean that area.
context_question: Patient has pain between vaginal lips and thigh, especially when using soap.
answer: The pain may be due to skin irritation from soaps or friction. Switch to a gentle, fragrance-free soap and avoid tight clothing. Keep the area dry and clean.

question: I am a 32-year-old female, suffering from fever and chills for four days. The fever on the first day subsided after taking Dexa and Para injections.

medicament: Ultracet (37.5 mg Tramadol hydrochloride and 325 mg Acetaminophen)

question: I am six weeks and six days pregnant. My urine test is positive and beta hCG is also increasing but I am very upset about my ultrasound report.
context_question: Six weeks and six days pregnant, positive urine test, increasing beta hCG, upset about ultrasound report showing no yolk sac or fetal pole.
answer: Continuous brown discharge is concerning. Avoid activities that could trigger a miscarriage. Monitor symptoms and have a scan after 10 weeks.
article_title: Role of Yolk Sac in Pregnancy

question: My pregnant wife, who is nine weeks and three days pregnant, fell and injured her back on the toilet seat yesterday, and she began bleeding from her vagina. This morning, the blood has b
context_question: Wife is nine weeks and three days pregnant, fell and is bleeding from vagina.
answer: Vaginal bleeding after a fall can indicate trauma or a threatened miscarriage. Immediate medical attention is necessary to ensure the safety of the baby.
article_title: Non Pregnancy-Related Abnormal Vaginal Bleeding - Causes, and Treatment

question: I am pregnant and sharing my ultrasound report with you. My early five-week transabdominal ultrasound shows an irregular gestational sac and a perfect yolk sac.
context_question: Five weeks pregnant, ultrasound shows irregular gestational sac and perfect yolk sac, no bleeding or pain.
answer: Irregular gestational sac needs monitoring. Continue taking 400 mg Progesterone and Baby Aspirin 81 mg. Wait for two weeks for another ultrasound.

question: I am 30 years old and approximately five weeks pregnant. However, I had a copper intrauterine device (IUD) in place at the time of conception.
context_question: 30 years old, five weeks pregnant with a copper IUD still in place.
answer: Pregnancy with an IUD is uncommon. The IUD should ideally be removed early if safe to do so, as it increases risks of miscarriage and infection.

question: My last period was a month back. I got beta hCG done due to symptoms two days back and the result was 15.6 mIU/mL.
context_question: Last period a month ago, beta hCG result was 15.6 mIU/mL, now experiencing bleeding.
answer: Beta hCG levels indicate possible pregnancy. If bleeding occurs, a repeat beta hCG and transvaginal scan are necessary to assess the situation.
article_title: Pregnancy and Birth

question: I have been pregnant for 23 weeks with signs of threatened abortion and am currently on bed rest without any medication or fluid administered. What should I do?
context_question: 23 weeks pregnant, signs of threatened abortion, on bed rest without medication.
answer: Seek immediate medical attention. Threatened abortion requires monitoring and treatment. An ultrasound is necessary to check the baby's condition.
article_title: Anemia - Symptoms, Causes, Prevention, and Treatment

question: I am in the fifth week of pregnancy. I am worried because after intercourse, I saw some vaginal bleeding with feeble cramps on the left side of abdomen and in lower back.
context_question: Fifth week pregnant, experienced vaginal bleeding and cramps after intercourse.
answer: Bleeding after intercourse may indicate decidual separation. An urgent transvaginal ultrasound is needed to rule out threatened miscarriage.

question: I went in for my anatomy scan at 18 weeks. The scan lasted about 30 minutes.
context_question: 18 weeks pregnant, anatomy scan lasted 30 minutes, all seemed normal.
answer: Everything appears normal. Suboptimal evaluation may be due to the baby's position. Relax and wait for the reports.

question: I underwent an anomaly scan today, and the doctors found that the baby’s left lateral ventricle measured 8.6 mm and mildly dilated.
context_question: Underwent anomaly scan, baby’s left lateral ventricle measured 8.6 mm and mildly dilated.
answer: Mild ventriculomegaly is usually not detrimental. Serial ultrasound monitoring is necessary to track any changes in size.

question: I have been pregnant for the last five months. I got an anomaly scan, and the doctor told me the right side of the baby's femur was thin.
context_question: Five months pregnant, anomaly scan showed thin right side of baby's femur.
answer: Thin femur may indicate a potential issue with bone development. Further evaluation and tests may be necessary.

question: I am approximately four weeks pregnant based on the date of conception. I have rheumatoid arthritis and take Methotrexate 10 mg a week, along with Folic acid 10 mg.
context_question: Approximately four weeks pregnant, taking Methotrexate 10 mg weekly and Folic acid 10 mg.
answer: Do not worry about Methotrexate exposure. Continue Folic acid and consider a fetal biophysical profile to check for abnormalities.

question: I am 21 years old. My weight is 105 lbs.
context_question: 21 years old, 105 lbs, 3 months 2 weeks pregnant, worried about chickenpox.
answer: Chickenpox carries a minimal risk to the fetus. Abortion is not necessary unless you choose to. Consider an anomaly scan at 18-19 weeks.

question: I am a female, and I am primigravida (17 weeks and 6 days). According to my LMP, I am 17 weeks and 6 day pregnant but according to my NT scan (done in 12 to 13 week )-gestation period sh
context_question: Primigravida, 17 weeks and 6 days pregnant, discrepancy between LMP and NT scan.
answer: Weeks will be counted according to the scan date. TIFFA scan can be done from 18 to 24 weeks for clear results.
article_title: Abnormal Fetal Lie - An Overview

question: We have done sonography during the sixth month of pregnancy. The report says there is a unilateral mild ventriculomegaly of 12 mm.
context_question: Sixth month of pregnancy, sonography report shows unilateral mild ventriculomegaly of 12 mm.
answer: Mild ventriculomegaly is usually not detrimental. Serial ultrasound monitoring is necessary to track any changes in size.
article_title: Ultrasound - Indications, Types, and Method

question: My wife is 22 weeks pregnant. Anomaly scan results show an aberrant right subclavian artery found in my baby's heart.
context_question: My wife is 22 weeks pregnant. Anomaly scan shows an aberrant right subclavian artery in the baby's heart. Karyotyping results seem normal. What treatment is available?
answer: Aberrant right subclavian artery (ARSA) is associated with Down syndrome. If karyotyping is normal, no immediate action is needed. Consult a pediatric cardiologist for further insight. If symptomatic, surgery may be required later.
article_title: Down Syndrome - Types , Causes, Symptoms, and Diagnosis

question: I am 28 weeks pregnant. All my fetal scans taken so far show that my baby has only one kidney, which is the left one, and it is working normally.
context_question: I am 28 weeks pregnant. Fetal scans show my baby has only one kidney, the left one, which is functioning normally. I doubt if there is a left kidney somewhere.
answer: Many children with a single kidney live normally, but there are risks of high blood pressure, urinary tract infections, and decreased kidney function. Follow-up scans and consultations with a pediatric nephrologist are recommended.
article_title: High Blood Pressure (Hypertension): Symptoms, Causes, and Treatment

question: My wife is seven weeks pregnant. She had to undergo an X-ray and mammogram recently.
context_question: My wife is seven weeks pregnant. She underwent an X-ray and mammogram recently. Is there any risk of radiation to the fetus?
answer: A single exposure to radiation from an X-ray or mammogram typically does not cause long-term effects on the fetus. An early anomaly scan at 12 weeks can help rule out abnormalities.

question: I am a 29-year-old pregnant woman and went through an anomaly scan. An anomaly scan showed hypoplastic nasal bone in the baby that might lead to Down syndrome.
context_question: I am a 29-year-old pregnant woman. Anomaly scan showed hypoplastic nasal bone in the baby, which might indicate Down syndrome. The NT scan showed a normal nasal bone.
answer: Hypoplastic nasal bone is one marker for Down syndrome. If it's the only anomaly and NT scan showed low risk, the baby may be normal. Further analysis of the scans is recommended.

question: I am a 35 year old female. I am 22 weeks and two days pregnant and was told at my last scan that the baby is about 10 days behind in size.
context_question: I am 35 years old and 22 weeks pregnant. My last scan indicated the baby is 10 days behind in size, but other readings were normal.
answer: Discrepancies in fetal age assessments via ultrasound are common. The difference is within limits. Follow-up scans may not be necessary as the fetus is not actually lagging behind.

question: The only abnormality in my anomaly scan was a newborn with a single umbilical artery. Could you please assist me in addressing my questions here?
context_question: The anomaly scan showed a single umbilical artery. Do babies with this condition develop normally? Will there be future health problems?
answer: A single umbilical artery may be isolated without additional disorders. About 70-80% of children with this condition will lead healthy lives. Monitor weight gain based on gestational age.

question: I missed my periods for the last four months. So, I had Deviry 10 mg two tablets a day for five days, but I did not get periods.
context_question: I missed my periods for four months and took Deviry 10 mg for five days but did not get my period. I then took Krimson 35 for 21 days and tested positive for pregnancy.
answer: Deviry supports pregnancy, while Krimson 35 is not advised during pregnancy. Since you are four months pregnant, a quadruple test and ultrasound are recommended to check for fetal growth anomalies.

question: I am in an unplanned pregnancy of 6 weeks. Two to three weeks after conceiving, I unknowingly drank wine when I was highly stressed.
context_question: I am 6 weeks pregnant and drank wine unknowingly two to three weeks after conceiving. My doctor is concerned about potential risks.
answer: The MMR vaccine poses a risk during pregnancy. The decision to continue or terminate the pregnancy is personal. Review the details and consider the potential risks.
article_title: MMR Vaccine - Uses and Side Effects

question: I am a 29 year old female, and I was hospitalized for a week due to UTI. As a part of it, a CT scan was taken, which showed that I am completely cured.
context_question: I am a 29-year-old female, hospitalized for UTI. A CT scan showed I am cured. I am now pregnant and worried about the CT scan's effect on the baby.
answer: CT scans use strong radiation, which can have adverse effects on the fetus. Consult a gynecologist for further advice on your situation.
article_title: Role of Multidetector Computed Tomography

question: I am on birth control pill Femilon for the last three months and I have followed 21/7 regime from the last three months.
context_question: I have been taking Femilon for three months with a 21/7 regime. My gynecologist advised a 21/5 regime for better efficacy. Should I continue with 21/7 or switch?
answer: Continue with the 21/7 pill-free cycle to avoid confusion. The hormone control remains the same regardless of the pill-free days. Follow the prescribed regimen for effective results.

question: I am a 24 year old female. I am approximately 209 lbs weight.
context_question: I am a 24-year-old female weighing approximately 209 lbs. I need emergency contraception due to failed birth control.
answer: Emergency contraceptive pills may have a rare chance of failure at your weight. Consider taking a low dose birth control pill like Novelon as an alternative.

question: We are newly married and going for honeymoon. I want to know some preventive measure except condom.
context_question: We are newly married and going on our honeymoon. I want to know about preventive measures besides condoms. I have PCOD.
answer: Consider low-dose oral contraceptive pills like Femilon starting from day 2 of your cycle. Alternatively, a copper-T IUCD can be placed if you prefer not to use condoms.

question: I would like to ask you a few things related to I-pill. Two years, I have taken around 10 to 12 I-pill tablets in eight months.
context_question: I have taken 10 to 12 I-pill tablets in eight months. What will be the side effects in the future? I have gained weight.
answer: I-pill contains high hormones for emergency contraception. Frequent use can lead to irregular cycles and other issues. Switch to regular contraceptive pills for better protection.
article_title: I-Pill - Uses, Dosage, Side Effects, Drug Warnings, and Precautions

question: I got married last month. Since my period date was clashing with my wedding date, I had to take some medicine in order to postpone my period.
context_question: I got married last month and took Deviry to postpone my period. My cycle is usually 27 or 28 days, but I expected my period to arrive early.
answer: You should have your period within 10 days after stopping Deviry. If you have taken the tablets correctly, you will get your period soon. Monitor for any ovarian cysts.

question: My marriage is ahead in 13 days. We are planning for honeymoon after four days of our marriage.
context_question: My marriage is in 13 days, and we plan to honeymoon four days after. We need help postponing my fiance's period and about birth control.
answer: Start taking Novelon from day one of this cycle and continue without a gap. This will help postpone her period until after the honeymoon.

question: I had sexual intercourse 31 days back and took three pregnancy tests until today morning, which were all negative.
context_question: I had intercourse 31 days ago, took three negative pregnancy tests, and started Cerazette a week later. I got my period but am worried it could be implantation bleeding.
answer: Implantation bleeding is usually light, but you had proper bleeding. Since tests are negative, there's no reason to worry. Cerazette does not affect pregnancy test results.
article_title: Vaginal Ring - Types, Advantages, and Risks

question: I and my fiance aged 23 are trying to figure out which oral contraceptive should we go for.
context_question: I and my 23-year-old fiance are looking for an oral contraceptive. She has no medical conditions but a family history of diabetes.
answer: Novelon oral contraceptive pills would suit her best. She should get her fasting blood sugar and blood pressure checked before starting.

question: I have been taking Femilon for the past six months as a regular contraception. I finished the 21 days of tablet and the last tablet was four days back and had unprotected intercourse two
context_question: Patient has taken Femilon for six months and had unprotected intercourse four days after the last tablet. Inquires about the need for emergency contraceptives.
answer: Femilon is an effective contraceptive, providing protection even during pill-free days. No need for extra precautions if taken regularly. Do not take I-pill. Start a new pack from the first day of the cycle after a five-day gap.

question: I am 25 and have had acne since my teenage years. It was severe at age 18 and responded well to two courses of Roaccutane.
context_question: Patient, 25, has had acne since teenage years, severe at 18, treated with Roaccutane. Acne returned after a few years.
answer: Recurring acne can be due to various factors. Roaccutane and the Mini pill helped previously, but they are not for long-term use. Identifying triggers is essential for better control over breakouts.
article_title: Precautions for the Safe Use of Isotretinoin (Accutane)

question: I had irregular periods. So, my family doctor suggested to have Femilon.
context_question: Patient had irregular periods and was advised to take Femilon. Stopped it three months ago and took two I-pills, now periods started early.
answer: Early periods are due to I-pill effects. No action needed, but continue with Femilon for contraception.

question: I had my last period a month ago. I had unprotected sexual intercourse with my partner three days later, on the third day of my last period.
context_question: Patient had unprotected intercourse three days after last period, which lasted two to three days.
answer: No need to worry; ovulation typically occurs between the 13th and 16th days. No pregnancy is likely. Other factors may cause missed periods; consider checking thyroid and prolactin levels.

question: I have been prescribed Prednisolone 60 mg for anti-inflammatory issues by my doctor. Is there a risk for me to become addicted to Prednisolone 60 mg taken once a day for a month?
context_question: Patient prescribed Prednisolone 60 mg for anti-inflammatory issues, inquires about addiction risk.
answer: Prednisolone dosage is based on body weight. Long-term use can lead to dependence; gradual tapering is necessary to avoid side effects.

question: I am spending sleepless nights. I am struggling to fall asleep and often get disturbed by dreams. I feel restless legs throughout the night. The next morning when I wake up I feel fatigue.
context_question: Patient struggles with sleep, experiences restless legs, and feels fatigued in the morning. Addicted to Benadryl for sleep.
answer: Dependence on Benadryl may be causing your symptoms. It is an OTC cough syrup with addiction potential. Consider seeking help to manage sleep issues without it.
article_title: Why Am I Yawning So Much? Causes and Solutions

question: I have been addicted to Codeine syrup for the last 14 years, and I would take three to four syrups daily.
context_question: Patient has been addicted to Codeine syrup for 14 years, experienced adverse side effects, and had a mild stroke last week.
answer: Continued use of Codeine is dangerous, especially after a stroke. Immediate cessation is crucial for your health. Seek help from a de-addiction center.

question: I recently learned about the risks of a drug overdose, particularly with opioids such as Fentanyl.
context_question: Patient is concerned about drug overdose risks with opioids like Fentanyl and inquires about precautions.
answer: To prevent overdose, store medications securely, follow prescription guidelines, and dispose of unused drugs properly. Education on drug safety is essential.

question: I have secondary dysmenorrhea and have miscarried twice before finding out this time I was pregnant. I have been using meth now.
context_question: Patient has secondary dysmenorrhea, miscarried twice, and is currently using meth. Feels guilty about drug use during pregnancy.

question: My mother has been suffering from stomach and back pain for the last seven months. She is taking painkiller injections and medicines daily.
context_question: Patient's mother has stomach and back pain for seven months and is taking painkillers daily, suspected addiction.
answer: Understanding the cause of pain is crucial. Not all painkillers are addictive. Gather detailed information about her pain and medications for better assessment.

question: As a 40-year-old man, I am increasingly concerned about the reports of Xylazine being mixed with opioids and causing overdoses.
context_question: Patient, 40, is concerned about Xylazine mixed with opioids and its dangers, inquires about its interaction and role in overdose prevention.
answer: Xylazine is a veterinary sedative that can increase overdose risks when mixed with opioids. Education and targeted interventions are necessary to address this issue.

question: I am considering compounded Semaglutide but am worried about long-term effects. How long can I safely take it?
context_question: Patient is considering compounded Semaglutide for weight loss and inquires about long-term safety and potential weight regain after stopping.
answer: Compounded Semaglutide can be safe for long-term use under medical supervision. Stopping may lead to weight regain as it suppresses appetite and aids blood sugar control.

question: I am a 19-year-old girl and I have been battling with depression for years. Last night was my fourth attempt at suicide.
context_question: Patient, 19, has battled depression for years and attempted suicide four times. Expresses feelings of hopelessness and trust issues.
answer: Immediate psychiatric help is necessary. Consider hospitalization for treatment with antidepressants and therapy to improve coping and problem-solving skills.

question: I am a little embarrassed but I am a user of ice. It has been 12 hours since I have had any.
context_question: Patient is a user of ice (Crystal Methamphetamine) and has not used it for 12 hours. Reports hot and red skin, weakness, headache, and hard breathing.
answer: ICE can cause severe health issues. Symptoms may be due to vasoconstriction. Visit the emergency department for evaluation of blood pressure and heart rate.

question: I am here because I have been dealing with a few health issues for about 2 years now. I feel weird and unusual sensations in my brain.
context_question: Patient has experienced unusual brain sensations for two years, describes it as a bubble feeling and pins and needles.
answer: Symptoms may be related to medication changes. Consider behavioral therapies to manage symptoms and discuss medication adjustments with your healthcare provider.

question: I am a male, 6 feet and 150 pounds. I am not on any medication.
context_question: Patient is a male, 6 feet tall, 150 pounds, not on medication, inquires about surgery after using Meth.
answer: Inform your doctor about Meth use before surgery. It may require postponement or adjustment of anesthetic dosage. Sharing this information is crucial.

context_question: Patient took 54 mg of Concerta and smoked weed, slept for 3-4 hours, then experienced low mood, took Sulpiride, and had uneven heartbeat and heartburn.
answer: It is advisable to stop smoking cigarettes and weed as they increase heartburn. You are on a high dose of Concerta (54 mg), and should avoid caffeine and smoking to prevent palpitations.

question: I was prescribed Etizolam 0.5 mg and Escitalopram for a month, as I had insomnia, stress, and anxiety. However, during the last week, it did not seem to work.
context_question: Patient prescribed Etizolam 0.5 mg and Escitalopram for insomnia, stress, and anxiety, but it hasn't worked in the last week. Concerned about addiction and dosage.
answer: Etizolam is not a sleep medicine but an anxiolytic. Long-term use is not advisable due to tolerance and addiction potential. Discuss with your psychiatrist about possibly increasing Escitalopram or adding short-term Clonazepam or Lorazepam.

question: I have been using Amphetamine for 25 years orally, which started as an energy boost as I have a hyperactive child. Could this be a health risk if I have never abused quantity (meaning my intake has never increased)?
context_question: Patient has used Amphetamine for 25 years for energy, concerned about health risks despite not abusing the quantity.

question: I have been taking medicines for schizophrenia for the last 16 years. I could not stop it earlier because my father was adamant about this.
context_question: Patient has taken medicines for schizophrenia for 16 years, feels addicted, and struggles with withdrawal effects.
answer: If you are symptom-free, discuss with your doctor about gradually reducing the dose of Quetiapine. It can be reduced by around 25 mg every two weeks until stopped completely in 10 to 12 weeks.

question: I am reaching out regarding my 55-year-old wife, who has been diagnosed with vulvar lichen sclerosus for the past three years.
context_question: Patient's wife, 55 years old, diagnosed with vulvar lichen sclerosus for three years, condition has worsened despite treatment.
answer: Your wife's lichen sclerosus has progressed, leading to thinning, scarring, and discomfort. The primary treatment involves topical Clobetasol, but adjustments may be needed if symptoms persist.

question: I have lichen sclerosus and some dark spots around my clitoris. I have noticed they have progressed, and I am concerned about vulval cancer.
context_question: Patient has lichen sclerosus and dark spots around the clitoris, concerned about vulval cancer.
answer: Pictures show nothing abnormal. If you have itching, use Clotrimazole cream. If dark spots increase, consider a biopsy to rule out pathology.

question: I have tightness in my penis, along with some discoloration on the penis head. I get wound sometimes.
context_question: Patient has tightness and discoloration on the penis head, experiences wounds.
answer: You may have developed lichen sclerosis et atrophicus. Please provide a photo for assessment and treatment suggestions.

question: I have a confidential problem. I have had a white, raised patch on the backside of my penis for seven months.
context_question: Patient has a white, raised patch on the backside of the penis for seven months, tried Clobetasol Propionate 0.05 %, but it did not help.
answer: It appears to be lichen sclerosus, a chronic inflammatory condition. If circumcised, consider other possibilities like vitiligo. Provide more details and a clinical photograph for assessment.

question: I am a 35-year-old unmarried woman. I have a problem with my vagina.
context_question: 35-year-old unmarried woman has a problem with her vagina, feels the vaginal hole is becoming too small.
answer: The sudden narrowing of the vaginal opening could be due to pelvic floor spasm, scarring, or hormonal changes. Possible causes include lichen sclerosus or vaginismus. Consult your doctor for further evaluation.

context_question: Patient has a severe infection on the penis head diagnosed as Zoon's balanitis, recurring for six years, causing skin loss and urinary blockage.
answer: Please send a picture of your lesion for proper diagnosis. Avoid applying steroids. Being circumcised is beneficial.

question: Last year my skin complexion changed on my hand, and then it spread up to my elbow.
context_question: Patient's skin complexion changed on hand, spread to elbow, and is now painful. A biopsy test was advised and results were fine.
answer: Please provide a picture of your hand and the biopsy report for further assessment. Clarify the nature of the complexion change.

question: I have had phimosis as long as I can remember.
context_question: Patient has had phimosis for a long time, worsening in 20s, with a tight foreskin opening of about 4 mm.

question: I was diagnosed with balanitis.
context_question: Patient diagnosed with balanitis, had itching and redness on the tip of the penis, treated with antifungal capsule and ointment.
answer: This seems to be recurrent balanitis. If sugar levels are normal, it may be due to persistent infection. Check if you can retract the foreskin fully.

question: I have been having some vaginal problems for the past five months.
context_question: Patient has vaginal problems for five months, starting with a tiny tear after sex, now with redness and inflammation.
answer: You have been on Tetracycline for acne. Long-term use could lead to resistance. Monitor your health and avoid over-treatment.

question: I have been diagnosed with lichen sclerosus around my vulva a few months ago through biopsy.
context_question: Patient diagnosed with lichen sclerosus around vulva, using Dermol 500 lotion and Dermovate cream, developed a painful cut.

question: I have had a white spot on the foreskin of my penis for two years that is growing in size and becoming whiter.
context_question: Patient has a white spot on the foreskin for two years, growing and itchy, concerned about lichen sclerosus or vitiligo.

question: I have a black patch on my penis tip and no other issues.
context_question: Patient has a black patch on the penis tip, thought it would go away but it has not.
answer: Such black patches can have multiple causes. Please send a photo for a definitive diagnosis and treatment suggestions.

question: I have histologically confirmed lichen sclerosus, and was circumcised. Upon visiting my general physician, he was not able to see some redness I, myself, had noticed in the fossa navicul
context_question: Patient has lichen sclerosus and noticed redness in the fossa navicularis. Seeking expert opinion on further healthcare.
answer: The redness should be checked by a specialist as it may indicate active LS-related inflammation or early scarring changes. Follow up is important even if subtle.
